rulings.Case Title: CAPITAL ONE BANK VS. QI H. HUANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Ntc Of Oppos. To Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 14, 2011, Line 1, Hearing On Claim Of Exemption (of claimant Qi H. Huang). Judgment Debtor's Claim of Exemption is granted. Judgment Debtor's monthly expenses are $3450.14, including the mortgages and car payment listed on page 2 of the Financial Statement. Even including Judgment Debtor's spouse's income, Judgment Debtor has shown that most of her monthly income is needed for her support. Beginning December 1, 2011, Judgment Debtor shall make monthly payments of $100 towards the judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DISCOVER BANK VS. LISA P. CHOW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing On Claim Of Exemption (Of Claimant Lisa P. Chow) Continued From 10/31/11
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 14, 2011, Line 8, Hearing On Claim Of Exemption (Of Claimant Lisa P. Chow). Denied in part. The judgment debtor failed to show that her entire monthly income is needed for her support. The levying officer is directed to release $350 to the judgment creditor for payment on the judgment. The remaining amount of $690.23 shall be released to the judgment debtor. Beginning December 1, 2011, judgment debtor shall make monthly payments of $350 on the judgment until it is satisfied in full. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: NORTH BEACH RETAIL ASSOCIATES, LLC, A LIMITED VS. HAMLET BET SARGHEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Setting Aside And Vacating Default Judgment Of Jul 14, 2011
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, November 14, 2011, line 7, DEFENDANTS VAHE BADALIAN, RAZMIK OHANJANIAN, JILBERT TAHMAZIAN AND HAMLET BET-SARGHEZ' MOTION For Order Setting Aside And Vacating Default Judgment Of Jul 14, 2011 is denied. Defendants have not set forth any justification for relied. There is no attorney affidavit of fault. CCP Section 473(b). Defendants neither diligently pursued nor timely moved to set aside entry of default or default judgment. (CCP § 473(b).) In addition, defendants have not set forth a meritorious defense to the action, or a justifiable excuse for failure to respond to the complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ANGUS M. CAMERON VS. SHARON L. HAMILTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 14, 2011, Line 7, PLAINTIFF ANGUS CAMERON's MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND EXPENSES. Denied. The proof of service does not show that P served the notice of the motion to compel on nonparty deponent Dr. Francene Colvin. (CCP § 2025.480(c); Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.1346.) The motion is untimely. (CCP § 2025.480(b).) = (302/MJM)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 14, 2011, Line 21, DEFENDANT ENCORE BANK, N.A., A NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, AS MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
